intro: The clearest difference between Census and Steampipe is pricing — Census is freemium (has a free tier), while Steampipe is free. Beyond that, lock-in — Census has medium lock-in (Reverse ETL, SQL-based configs portable), whereas Steampipe has low lock-in (Open-source SQL for cloud). Census fits SOC 2 / ISO 27001 preparation; Steampipe fits SaaS companies pursuing SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA. On the strengths side, Census is cited for SOC 2 / ISO 27001 preparation, while Steampipe is cited for continuous evidence collection saves audit preparation time. The honest trade-off: Census is the wrong call for pre-revenue startups; Steampipe is the wrong call for projects not subject to compliance frameworks; Census users flag pre-revenue startups, and Steampipe users flag initial control mapping requires significant setup effort. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap. On developer experience the data shows Census at 4/5 and Steampipe at 5/5, with transparency at 5/5 and 5/5 respectively.
quick_take: Census is for SOC 2 / ISO 27001 preparation; Steampipe is for SaaS companies pursuing SOC 2, ISO; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Census if your use case is SOC 2 / ISO 27001 preparation, and a freemium (has a free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than free.
verdict_b: Choose Steampipe if your use case is SaaS companies pursuing SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA, and a free pricing shape fits your budget better than freemium (has a free tier).

faq.a: On listed pricing Steampipe is free, whereas Census follows a freemium model, so Steampipe is cheaper on the sticker. Factor in self-hosting and maintenance time before concluding which is actually cheaper for your team. Check both pricing pages for current tiers.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Census to Steampipe?
faq.a: Migrating from Census to Steampipe is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Moving off Census is harder because of medium lock-in (Reverse ETL, SQL-based configs portable). Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, Steampipe is rated 5/5 and Census 4/5, so Steampipe edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is Steampipe a good alternative to Census?
faq.a: Steampipe is a reasonable alternative to Census when Steampipe fits SaaS companies pursuing SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA. If your use case matches Census's sweet spot — SOC 2 / ISO 27001 preparation — staying with Census is probably safer. Census itself is not meant for pre-revenue startups, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
